title: DFU Flow: Device Disconnected on Software Update Page — Should Block Navigation to Three Easy Steps
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
Ensure a Poly device (e.g., Voyager Surround 85) is connected via Bluetooth.
Tap the device name (e.g., 'Voyager Surround 85') to open Device Details screen.
Navigate to 'Software & Updates' under the Device section.
If a firmware update is available, the Software Update page displays with a 'Continue' or 'Update' button. If no update is available, note this and use a device with a pending update.
While on the Software Update page (before tapping Continue), disable Bluetooth on the phone OR power off the device to simulate disconnection.
Wait 5 seconds for the app to detect the disconnection (observe the device status change to 'Disconnected' or 'Not Connected').
Tap the 'Continue' button on the Software Update page.
expected: The app MUST NOT navigate to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. Instead, the app should display an error message such as 'Device disconnected. Please reconnect to continue with the update.' or block the flow with a dialog stating 'Device is no longer connected. Please reconnect your device.' The user should remain on the Software Update page or be redirected to the device list.

title: DFU Flow: Reconnect Device After Disconnect on Software Update Page — Validate Flow Recovery
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect a Poly device (e.g., Voyager Free 60).
Navigate to Device Details → Software & Updates.
Ensure a firmware update is available and you are on the Software Update page.
Disconnect the device by turning off Bluetooth on the phone.
Wait for the app to show 'Disconnected' status.
Re-enable Bluetooth and reconnect the device (put device in pairing mode if needed, or simply re-enable BT if auto-reconnect occurs).
Once the device shows as 'Connected' again, tap 'Continue' on the Software Update page.
expected: After reconnection, tapping 'Continue' should successfully proceed to the 'Three Easy Steps' page (or the next step in the DFU flow). The app should correctly re-evaluate the device connection state and allow the flow to proceed only when the device is confirmed connected.

title: DFU Flow: App Backgrounding While on Software Update Page with Disconnected Device — State Persistence
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect a Poly device (e.g., Voyager Surround 85).
Navigate to Device Details → Software & Updates → Software Update page.
Disconnect the device by turning off Bluetooth.
Press the Home button to background the app.
Wait 30 seconds.
Reopen the app from the recent apps list.
Observe the current screen and device connection status.
Tap 'Continue' on the Software Update page.
expected: After reopening the app, the device should still show as 'Disconnected'. Tapping 'Continue' must NOT navigate to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. The app should display an appropriate error or block the flow due to the disconnected device state. The disconnected state must persist across app backgrounding.

title: DFU Flow: App Force-Kill While on Software Update Page with Disconnected Device — State Persistence
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect a Poly device (e.g., Voyager Free 60).
Navigate to Device Details → Software & Updates → Software Update page.
Disconnect the device by turning off Bluetooth.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe away from recent apps).
Reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Observe the initial screen (should be device list or home screen).
Reconnect the device if not auto-connected.
Navigate back to Device Details → Software & Updates.
Tap 'Continue' on the Software Update page.
expected: The app should start fresh after force-kill. When navigating back to Software & Updates and tapping 'Continue', the app should correctly check the current device connection state. If the device is now connected, the flow should proceed normally. If still disconnected, the app must block navigation to 'Three Easy Steps'.

title: DFU Flow: Phone Rotation While on Software Update Page with Disconnected Device — Configuration Change Handling
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect a Poly device (e.g., Voyager Surround 85).
Navigate to Device Details → Software & Updates → Software Update page.
Disconnect the device by turning off Bluetooth.
Rotate the phone from portrait to landscape (or vice versa).
Observe the screen after rotation — note if the Software Update page is preserved.
Tap 'Continue' on the Software Update page.
expected: After rotation, the app should preserve the Software Update page state and the disconnected device status. Tapping 'Continue' must NOT navigate to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. The app should block the flow and show an appropriate error message indicating the device is disconnected.

title: DFU Flow: Device Disconnection During DFU on Voyager Surround 85 (Over-the-Ear Headset) — Validate Blocking Behavior
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect a Voyager Surround 85 headset.
Navigate to Device Details → Software & Updates.
Ensure a firmware update is available.
While on the Software Update page (before tapping Continue), disconnect the headset by powering it off.
Wait 5 seconds for the app to update the connection status.
Tap 'Continue' on the Software Update page.
expected: The app must detect the Voyager Surround 85 is disconnected and block navigation to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. An error dialog or message should appear: 'Device disconnected. Please reconnect to proceed with the update.' The user must not be able to proceed further in the DFU flow without a connected device.

title: DFU Flow: Device Disconnection During DFU on Poly Free 60 (Earbuds) — Validate Blocking Behavior
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect a pair of Poly Free 60 earbuds.
Navigate to Device Details → Software & Updates.
Ensure a firmware update is available.
While on the Software Update page (before tapping Continue), place both earbuds in the charging case and close the lid (or disable Bluetooth on the phone).
Wait 5 seconds for the app to detect disconnection.
Tap 'Continue' on the Software Update page.
expected: The app must detect the Free 60 earbuds are disconnected and block navigation to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. An appropriate error message must be displayed. The DFU flow must not proceed without a connected device.

title: DFU Flow: Device Disconnection During DFU on Voyager Free 60+ (with Case) — Validate Blocking Behavior
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect a Voyager Free 60+ (with case connected via Bluetooth).
Navigate to Device Details → Software & Updates.
Ensure a firmware update is available.
While on the Software Update page (before tapping Continue), disconnect the device by turning off the case or disabling Bluetooth.
Wait 5 seconds for the app to detect disconnection.
Tap 'Continue' on the Software Update page.
expected: The app must detect the Voyager Free 60+ is disconnected and block navigation to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. An appropriate error message must be displayed. The DFU flow must not proceed without a connected device, regardless of device type (headset, earbuds, or case).

title: DFU Flow: Multiple Disconnect/Reconnect Cycles on Software Update Page — State Consistency
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect a Poly device (e.g., Voyager Surround 85).
Navigate to Device Details → Software & Updates → Software Update page.
Disconnect the device (disable Bluetooth).
Wait 5 seconds. Verify the app shows 'Disconnected'.
Reconnect the device (re-enable Bluetooth, wait for auto-reconnect).
Verify the app shows 'Connected'.
Disconnect the device again.
Wait 5 seconds.
Reconnect the device again.
Verify the app shows 'Connected'.
Tap 'Continue' on the Software Update page.
expected: After multiple disconnect/reconnect cycles, the app must correctly reflect the current connection state. When the device is connected and 'Continue' is tapped, the app should proceed to the 'Three Easy Steps' page. The app must not cache an old disconnected state and must always check the live connection status before proceeding.

title: DFU Flow: Verify 'Three Easy Steps' Page is Inaccessible Without Connected Device via Deep Link or Back Navigation
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app with NO device connected.
Attempt to directly navigate to the 'Three Easy Steps' page by any means (e.g., if a deep link exists, try to open it; or attempt to navigate back from a previous session state).

Alternatively: Connect a device, navigate to Software Update page, disconnect the device, then attempt to use Android's back button or gesture to navigate back to a previous screen and then forward again to see if the 'Three Easy Steps' page becomes accessible.
Observe the app behavior.
expected: The 'Three Easy Steps' page must be gated behind a connected device check. It must not be reachable via deep links, back navigation tricks, or any other means when no device is connected. The app should redirect to the device list or home screen if the user attempts to access this page without a connected device.
